Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト‥‥ですかな？ 貴方の指紋、というのは‥‥
English: So what is all this fuss about fingerprints anyway...?

Japanese: 哀牙さん‥‥。 きのうのこと、覚えてますよね？
English: Mr. Atmey... Do you recall the events of yesterday?

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 出番だよ。
English: Hey, Nick! Come on! Open it up!

Japanese: え！　マズいだろ 勝手にさわっちゃ！
English: Hey! Wait a minute! We can't just open his private property!

Japanese: いいって、いいって。 さっさと調査する。
English: Don't be such a fuddy-duddy! This is an important investigation!

Japanese: どう？　何が入ってる？
English: Well? What's in there?

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待てよ。 今、取り出すから‥‥
English: H-Hang on a sec. I'm taking it out now...

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥何か、つるつるで カタいもの‥‥みたいだな‥‥）
English: (Whatever it is, it feels... kinda hard... and smooth...)

Japanese: ‥‥あいや、しばらくッ！
English: ...Well, hello there!!

Japanese: あのとき‥‥たしかにぼくは、 バッグの中は見なかった。
English: It's true that I didn't get a chance to look in the bag at that time.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But!

Japanese: その中のモノに、 さわっていたんですよ。
English: I did touch what was inside.

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんと！ さ、“さわった”‥‥？
English: Wh-What!? Y-You touched it...?

Japanese: キオクに残っていますからね。 “つるつるでカタいものだった”
English: And I remember it very well. It was "smooth and hard".

Japanese: あ‥‥あいや‥‥ それは、しばらく‥‥
English: W-Well, err... Th-That was just...

Japanese: 裁判長！　そのツボの 指紋を調べてください！
English: Your Honor! I'd like the court to examine the fingerprints on that urn.

Japanese: もし、ぼくの指紋が ついていれば‥‥
English: If my fingerprints are on there...

Japanese: あのツボは、哀牙探偵の 事務所にあったことになります！
English: Then it proves that the urn was in Detective Atmey's office!

Japanese: ツボに指紋が残っていたとして‥‥
English: Well, even if your fingerprints are on the urn...

Japanese: それが“いつ”付着したものか、 立証できるのかい？
English: ...it still doesn't prove when they were put there, does it?

Japanese: ‥‥もちろんです。
English: Of course it does.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥
English: Wh-What did you say!?

Japanese: 秘宝展のセキニンシャ・華宮　霧緒 さんは、こう言っています。
English: Not what I said, it's what Adrian Andrews, the person in charge of the exhibition said.

Japanese: でも‥‥私、いっしょうけんめい ピカピカにみがいて、
English: I polished it until it was just about glowing...

Japanese: 少しでも高そうに見えるように がんばったんですよ！
English: I thought maybe I could make it look more valuable!

Japanese: ツボはピカピカにみがかれて、 すべての指紋が消されていました。
English: If she polished it that much, she must have removed any and all fingerprints on it.

Japanese: ぼくが、ツボに指紋を つけられるチャンスは‥‥
English: And the only chance I had to get my fingerprints on it after that was...

Japanese: きのう！　哀牙探偵事務所しか なかったんですよ！
English: Yesterday! At the Atmey Detective Agency!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　この、 ゴドー・ブレンド１０７号‥‥
English: Ha...! This blend... Godot Blend #107...

Japanese: ちょっとばかり‥‥ 苦味がすぎたようだぜ！
English: I've decided... It's a little too bitter after all!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 弁護人の要請を受け入れます！
English: Order! Order! Order! I accept the defense's request!

Japanese: 係官！　至急、このツボを‥‥
English: Bailiff! Take this urn and...

Japanese: あいやッ！
English: Wait!

Japanese: 待たれよ、ご老人。 ‥‥その必要はございませぬぞ。
English: Wait a moment, Your Honor! ...There's no need for that.

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人‥‥？
English: N-No need you say...?

Japanese: さよう‥‥そのツボには、 弁護士殿の指紋が残っている‥‥
English: Precisely... I already know Mr. Wright's fingerprints are on the urn...

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What are you saying!

Japanese: （ついに‥‥打ち崩した！）
English: (Yes! I've finally broken him down!)

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっは！ はっはっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Hee hee hee ha ha ha Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Ho ho ho hee hee hee!

Japanese: ‥‥さあさあみなさん、 ごらんくださあい‥‥！
English: Take a good look, everyone!

Japanese: 天才にふさわしき好敵手を求めて、 ついに自ら犯罪に手を染めた‥‥
English: Unable to find a rival worthy of my genius, I was forced to create one by myself!

Japanese: あわれなピエロでござあい‥‥
English: Here I am! The tragic clown...

Japanese: ‥‥なんかモーレツに アブないよ、あのヒト‥‥
English: This guy is nuttier than a fruitcake...

Japanese: やあれ！　この我こそが、 かの仮面マスクだった！
English: You see! It was me all along! I am the one and only Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: さよう！　みなさん、 楽しんでいただけましたかな‥‥！
English: Ha ha! I hope you all enjoyed my little performance!

Japanese: あーっはっはっはっはっはっは！ はっはっはっはっはっはっ！
English: Hee hee hee ha ha ha Aha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Ho ho ho hee hee hee!

Japanese: それで‥‥ゴドー検事。 星威岳　哀牙は‥‥？
English: Well, Mr. Godot... What's Mr. Atmey's condition?

Japanese: 控え室で高笑いしているぜ。
English: He's still in the lobby, laughing insanely, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥オレも、あれぐらい 楽しそうに笑ってみたいもんだ。
English: I wish I could enjoy the joke as much as he seems to be.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ コタエが出たようですね。
English: Well, it looks like the matter has been settled.

Japanese: ワレワレはもう少しで、無実の 青年に汚名を着せるところでした。
English: I came perilously close to besmirching the record of an innocent young man.

Japanese: ‥‥おぞましき“怪人”の汚名を。
English: Besmirching him with the title of "thief"!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥ なるほどくんが正しかったんだね！
English: Nick, you were right after all!

Japanese: （‥‥‥優作くんは、 怪人じゃなかったんだ！）
English: (Yeah! I guess Mr. DeLite really wasn't the thief!)

Japanese: それでは、天杉　優作くんに 判決を言いわたします。
English: The court finds the defendant, Mr. Ron DeLite...

Japanese: ちがう！　だから、ちがうんです！ ‥‥‥‥いや。
English: You're wrong! Wrong I tell you! Err, umm, I mean...

Japanese: ちがう、っていうか、むしろ まちがってる、っていうか、それも
English: Not exactly wrong so much, but actually not right is what I was really trying to say

Japanese: （お、おいおい‥‥まさか‥‥）
English: (Oh no... He's not... This can't be happening...)

Japanese: その、おぞましき怪人の件 なんですけど‥‥
English: The thief... The sneaky, odious thief who's been stealing all the treasures...

Japanese: 怪人は、ボクなんです！ っていうか、ボク、怪人なんです！
English: It's me! I'm him! I'm the one you want! I'm the thief I tell you!

Japanese: だから‥‥有罪にしちゃって くださーーーい！
English: So do it...! Pronounce me guilty! Pleeeease!!

Japanese: 今までの審理は、いったい なんだったのでしょうか。
English: I don't know what kind of a kangaroo court you all think this is, but...

Japanese: 怪人の正体は立証されました！ 早いトコ、判決を‥‥
English: The true identity of the thief has already been proven! Please hurry and pass judg--

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ だって‥‥怪人はボクなんですッ！
English: What are you talking about!? I already confessed! I'm the thief I tell you!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事！ そこでコーヒーを飲まない！
English: ...Mr. Godot! Don't just stand there drinking coffee!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　おい、怪人さんよォ。
English: Ha...! Hey there, Mr. Thief...

Japanese: は、はい！　なんですか！
English: Y-Yes! Yes, sir!?

Japanese: アンタもオトコなら、 自分のケツは自分でふくコトだ。
English: If you're really a man, then clean up your own mess.

Japanese: あの‥‥。スミマセンが イミがわかりません。
English: I-I'm sorry... I'm afraid I just don't have any idea what you mean.

Japanese: テメエで証明するんだ。 ‥‥仮面マスクは自分だ、ってな。
English: If you are Mask☆DeMasque... then prove it. That's what it means.

Japanese: は‥‥はい！　よろこんで！
English: Y-Yes, sir! I'll be happy to!

Japanese: よろこんでるよ、なるほどくん‥‥
English: He says he'll be happy to, Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ‥‥ かなり、セツない。
English: It's kinda cute... He's 100% committed to his fantasy.

Japanese: よおし、イイ子だ。 ‥‥ただし！
English: Good boy! Just remember one thing.

Japanese: オトコになるチャンスは‥‥ いつだって、たった一度きりだぜ。
English: A boy only gets one chance in his life to become a man!

Japanese: も‥‥モチロンです！ ボク‥‥ボク、がんばります！
English: I-I know that! I-I won't fail! I swear!

Japanese: じゃあ、話してみな。 ‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: OK, then... Talk! ...We're all listening.

Japanese: ヤレヤレ‥‥しかたありませんな。 聞いてみるとしましょうか‥‥。
English: Oh well... Let's all have a listen to this "confession"...

Japanese: ～仮面マスクの正体～
English: -- Mask☆DeMasque's Identity --

Japanese: じつは、このボクが 怪人☆仮面マスクだったんです！
English: The truth is, I've been Mask☆DeMasque all along!

Japanese: ‥‥逆に、ボクが仮面マスクじゃ ないって、証明できますか！
English: I mean, you can't prove that I'm not actually Mask☆DeMasque, can you?

Japanese: ツボが盗まれた夜のアリバイだって ちゃーんと、ありませんしね！
English: I don't have an alibi for the night the urn was stolen, after all!

Japanese: あの晩、ボク、仮面マスクのカッコ をして、現場に舞いおりたんです。
English: I donned my costume that night and dancingly descended upon the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ホラ。写真にも写ってる じゃないですか！　このボクが。
English: Look! You can see right there in the photo! That's me!

Japanese: エンブレムなら‥‥ドアのふちに 引っかけて、取れちゃったんです！
English: As for my broach, I snagged it on the door handle and it got torn off, that's all!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。ヤッカイなことに なってきましたな‥‥
English: Hmm... I don't like the direction this trial has taken...

Japanese: （いつものことだけど‥‥）
English: (But this is how every trial goes... At least with me, anyway...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ やればできるじゃねえか。
English: Ha...! You're doing great!

Japanese: へへへ‥‥。 テレちゃいますね、なんか。
English: Hee hee hee... Stop it, Mr. Godot... You're embarrassing me.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤクソクどおり、アンタの 証言はこれ１回きりだ。
English: Like I said, you're only going to get one chance to testify, alright?

Japanese: ‥‥この証言を守り抜けば‥‥ オレもヤクソクは守るぜ。
English: But if you make it through this with flying colors, I'll keep my promise, too.

Japanese: 怪人☆仮面マスクとして、 アンタの拘留をつづけてやるさ。
English: I'll make sure you stay locked up in prison as the one and only true Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ヨロシクおねがいします！ ボク‥‥がんばります！
English: Thanks so much, Mr. Godot! I... I'll do my best!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは弁護人。 ゴクロウですが、尋問を。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. I'm afraid it's time for the cross-examination.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: ～仮面マスクの正体～
English: -- Mask☆DeMasque's Identity --

Japanese: じつは、このボクが 怪人☆仮面マスクだったんです！
English: The truth is, I've been Mask☆DeMasque all along!

Japanese: ‥‥逆に、ボクが仮面マスクじゃ ないって、証明できますか！
English: I mean, you can't prove that I'm not actually Mask☆DeMasque, can you?

Japanese: ツボが盗まれた夜のアリバイだって ちゃーんと、ありませんしね！
English: I don't have an alibi for the night the urn was stolen, after all!

Japanese: あの晩、ボク、仮面マスクのカッコ をして、現場に舞いおりたんです。
English: I donned my costume that night and dancingly descended upon the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ホラ。写真にも写ってる じゃないですか！　このボクが。
English: Look! You can see right there in the photo! That's me!

Japanese: エンブレムなら‥‥ドアのふちに 引っかけて、取れちゃったんです！
English: As for my broach, I snagged it on the door handle and it got torn off, that's all!

Japanese: なんで優作さん、ここまで 怪人になりたがるのかなあ。
English: Why does Mr. DeLite want to be Mask☆DeMasque so badly anyway? It's kinda disturbing.

Japanese: 目立ちたがり屋、ってワケでも なさそうなのに。
English: He doesn't seem like your typical attention whore to me.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくんとちがって。
English: Unlike, say... you, Nick.

Japanese: ぼくは目立ちたがり屋じゃないぞ。 （とはいえ‥‥）
English: I am nothing of the sort, thank you very much.

Japanese: （たしかに、どうも不自然だよな、 優作くんのタイドは‥‥）
English: (But I gotta admit it is kinda scary the way Ron acts...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥この証言の“穴”を 見つけるしかないね！
English: Anyway... Time to poke some holes in that testimony of his!

Japanese: でも‥‥、まれかさんのハナシ では、そう思いこんでいるだけ‥‥
English: You may think you're the real Mask☆DeMasque... but your wife thinks you're delusional.

Japanese: まれかちゃんには、まだ話して いないんです。ボクの正体‥‥
English: I haven't told Dessie yet... About my true identity, I mean...

Japanese: あの。いろいろ事情がありまして。
English: Believe me, I've got my reasons.

Japanese: （話さなくても、あの部屋を見れば わかりそうな気もするけど‥‥）
English: (With the way your room is decked out, how could she NOT know about it...?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。怪人にも、フクザツな 家庭の事情があるのですねえ。
English: Hmm... Even thieves have complicated family situations, I suppose.

Japanese: （どうする？　あまり、 カンケイなさそうだけど‥‥）
English: (What should I do? It sounds like we're about to get sidetracked... again.)

Japanese: どうして、奥さんに キチンと打ち明けないんですか？
English: So why are you withholding the truth from your wife?

Japanese: きっと、話しても 信じてくれないだろうし‥‥
English: Well... first of all, I don't think she'd believe me anyway...

Japanese: それに、まれかちゃん‥‥ 大ッキライなんです。
English: And second... If there's one thing that Dessie absolutely detests...

Japanese: ‥‥その。犯罪者が。
English: ...it's criminals.

Japanese: それならば、なぜ 怪人業を始めたのですかな？
English: If that's the case, then why did you ever start thieving to begin with?

Japanese: まれかちゃん。お金を 使うのが大好きで‥‥、
English: Well, Dessie is crazy about shopping. She's a real shopaholic...

Japanese: そしてボク、お金を使う まれかちゃんが好きだから‥‥
English: But she's the love of my life, Your Honor. I guess you could say I'm a "Dessie-aholic"...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。やはり、フクザツな 家庭の事情があるのですねえ。
English: Hmm... So you do have a complicated family situation after all.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいです。 証言をつづけてください。
English: ...Alright, that's enough. Please continue with your testimony. I beg of you.

Japanese: （ヒトの家庭にクチをつっこんでも しかたないか‥‥）
English: (There's no point in digging through any more of their dirty laundry...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥とりあえず、 証言をつづけてもらえますか。
English: Well, why don't you continue with your testimony for now.

Japanese: あの‥‥今は、きみが自分で 証明するハズでしょう。
English: Umm... I thought you were going to be the one to show US the proof.

Japanese: “怪人☆仮面マスクの正体が 天杉　優作である”‥‥って。
English: You know, that you're the real Mask☆DeMasque and not just some kind of obsessed fanboy.

Japanese: ‥‥な。なんでもボクに 押しつけないでくださーーーい！
English: Hey now, that's not fair. Why do I have to do all the hard work anywaaaaaay!?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Maybe because you're the one making the outrageous claim... for a change.)

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん。 優作さんにできるワケないって。
English: Come on, Nick! You know that Mr. DeLite couldn't have committed the crime!

Japanese: ここはあたしたちが、バシッと 証拠をたたきつけてあげないと。
English: You're the one with the fancy law degree! It's time to put it to work already!

Japanese: 《優作くんは仮面マスクじゃない》 ‥‥っていう証拠か。
English: I need some kind of proof that Ron couldn't possibly be Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: でも‥‥それがあれば、 苦労しないよね‥‥。
English: Well, I guess if you had that kind of proof, we wouldn't still be here, would we...?

Japanese: （とりあえず‥‥）
English: (For the time being, maybe I need to shift strategy...)

Japanese: （優作くんが、ツボを盗めなかった ‥‥そいつを立証してみるか）
English: (I should try to show that Mr. DeLite couldn't have stolen the urn.)

Japanese: とにかく、ボク‥‥
English: Anyway, I...

Japanese: アリバイ‥‥ですか。
English: You've got no alibi...?

Japanese: 私も、このシゴトはもう 長いのですが‥‥
English: I've been a judge for a long time...

Japanese: アリバイがないのをイバる 被告人は、初めてですな。
English: And this is the first time I've ever heard a defendant brag about having no alibi.

Japanese: だってボク、あの晩は高菱屋に いましたから！　‥‥‥‥いや。
English: But I tell you I was in Lordly Tailor that night! ...Errr, no.

Japanese: それじゃアイマイだな。ちゃんと、 高菱屋百貨店の地下倉庫って言って
English: That's too vague, even for me. To be more precise, I was down in the basement warehouse

Japanese: （待てよ‥‥）
English: (Hold on...)

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: 事件があった時間、 優作くんがどこにいたか‥‥？
English: Where was Ron DeLite when the crime happened anyway...?

Japanese: 彼のアリバイが立証できれば、 話はカンタンだな、って思って。
English: If we can prove he had an alibi after all, this case will be a piece of cake.

Japanese: そ、そりゃそうだけど‥‥
English: W-Well, you're right, but...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　アンタに そいつが立証できるってのかい？
English: Ha...! You think you can prove that? Wake up and smell the coffee!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: W-Well, I think maybe I can...

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。被告人のアリバイを 立証する、証拠があるのですかな？
English: Mr. Wright, do you have any evidence that shows the defendant has an alibi?

Japanese: あるワケないじゃないですか、 そんな証拠が！
English: Of course not! Don't be ridiculous!

Japanese: す、スミマセン！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: ‥‥って、なんで私に 怒るんですか！
English: ...Say! What am I apologizing for!?

Japanese: コラ、証人！　あなたが さっさと証言しないからです！
English: You! Witness! Hurry up with your testimony already!

Japanese: す、スミマセン！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、いっつもボクが とばっちりを食らっちゃうんだよな
English: ...Why is it that I'm always the one that has to apologize to everyone in the end

Japanese: ありますよ、証拠ぐらい！
English: I have the evidence! Or do you think I'm still some sort of third-rate rookie!?

Japanese: お。めずらしく強気ですね。
English: Oh...! I-I've never seen you this angry before!

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ。オトコは、ミジメな ときこそ強気でなきゃいけねえ。
English: ...I'm not surprised. Anger is the last refuge of the pathetic.

Japanese: （ベツに今は、とりたてて ミジメとも思えないけど‥‥）
English: (I thought I was more confident than angry...)

Japanese: それでは、提示して いただきましょうか。
English: Well then, let's see this evidence already.

Japanese: 被告人があの晩、どこにいたか？ ‥‥それを立証する証拠品を！
English: Show to this court evidence that proves where your client was on the night of the crime!

Japanese: その証拠は、コイツです！
English: Here's the evidence!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　そうさ、まるほどう。
English: Ha...! It looks like I was right. ...Mr. Trite.

Japanese: オトコはミジメなときほど、 指をつきつけなきゃいけねえ。
English: The more pathetic a man is, the more likely he is to start pointing fingers!

Japanese: （なんとしても、ぼくを ミジメにしたいらしいな‥‥）
English: For some reason, the good Prosecutor really wants to prove that I'm "pathetic"...

Japanese: ジッサイ、ミジメですからな、 この証拠品は。
English: That's because you are pathetic! And so is your "evidence"!

Japanese: （ぼくの見こみちがい だったか‥‥）
English: (Oops. I guess I was wrong... Again.)

Japanese: じゃあ、ボクのアリバイは やっぱり、なかったってコトで。
English: Aha! I told you I didn't have an alibi!

Japanese: 舞いおりた‥‥って、 どこからですか？
English: "Dancingly descended"...? From where exactly?

Japanese: え。それは、入り口のドアから ですけど、モチロン。
English: Well... From the entrance, I suppose. Where else?

Japanese: （“舞い”も“おりる”も カンケイないじゃないか！）
English: (So in actuality, he neither "danced" nor "descended"! ...Someone save me. Again.)

Japanese: やっぱり、“舞いおりる”って 言ったほうが、怪人っぽいからね。
English: I guess he thinks "descended" sounds more heroic or thief-like or something.

Japanese: と、とにかく！　ボクが現場に いたのは、ハッキリしてます！
English: A-Anyway! I was there at the crime scene! Just like I said!

Japanese: 哀牙探偵の防犯カメラが 撮影した写真ですね‥‥。
English: This is the photo that was taken by the security camera at the crime scene...

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、仮面マスクが 写っていますな。
English: No doubt about it. That's Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: しかし‥‥この怪人は、 さっきも問題になりました。
English: Yes, but don't forget there's something important missing.

Japanese: ホンモノの怪人は、ムネに エンブレムをつけているはずです！
English: The real Mask☆DeMasque has a big broach on his chest!

Japanese: ‥‥気がつかなかったんですか？ エンブレムが取れたとき。
English: You didn't notice it when your broach was torn off?

Japanese: え！　その‥‥キンチョーします からね。怪人やってると。
English: Huh!? W-Well... I'm always a little nervous when I'm working.

Japanese: （エンブレムのウラには、 衣装の切れはしがついていた）
English: (There's a bit of clothing left on the back side of the broach.)

Japanese: （そうカンタンに取れるとは 思えないけどな‥‥）
English: (I don't think it could have been torn off that easily...)

Japanese: でも‥‥それって、今は カンケイないんじゃないかな。
English: Yes, but... I don't see what that has to do with anything.

Japanese: 立証するのは《優作さんは 怪人じゃない》ってコトでしょ。
English: Right now we're trying to prove that Mr. DeLite isn't the thief, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。 （もう一度、証言を聞いてみるか）
English: Yeah, I guess so. (Let me hear that testimony one more time.)

Japanese: 被告人。‥‥これ、 モチロン知っていますね？
English: Mr. DeLite... I believe you've seen this before, correct?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！　それは‥‥！
English: Ah...! Th-That's...!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: 脅迫状です。 ‥‥そう書いてありますから。
English: A blackmail letter. That's what it looks like from the contents.

Japanese: きょうはくじょう‥‥
English: B-Blackmail...?

Japanese: これによると‥‥ 事件当夜の、午前１時。
English: According to this letter, at 1 o'clock in the morning on the night of the crime...

Japanese: 被告人・天杉　優作は、ＫＢ警備 という会社に呼び出されている！
English: ...Mr. Ron DeLite was ordered to go to the offices of KB Security!

Japanese: ご、午前１時‥‥？
English: 1 o'clock in the morning!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんです。 その時間は、まさに‥‥
English: Correct.

Japanese: 犯行写真が撮影された時刻です！
English: Which is around the time the security camera photo was taken!

Japanese: 午前１時に、この被告人が 警備会社にいたのならば‥‥
English: So if the defendant was at KB Security at 1 o'clock in the morning...

Japanese: そのアリバイは、カンゼンに 成立します！
English: ...then that proves that he has a watertight alibi!

Japanese: うわわうわわッ！
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: ちなみに、高菱屋百貨店から ＫＢ警備会社までのキョリは‥‥
English: Furthermore, considering the distance between Lordly Tailor and KB Security...

Japanese: クルマで約３０分もあるのです。 （‥‥と、矢張が言ってたな‥‥）
English: ...it would have taken 30 minutes to get there by car! (According to Larry anyway...)

Japanese: いかがですかッ、ゴドー検事！
English: Well, Mr. Godot!? Do you have anything to say?

Japanese: だから、コーヒーを飲まない！
English: And stop drinking that coffee!

Japanese: ‥‥オイ怪人。出番だぜ。 あの弁護士に、反論してやれよ！
English: ...Come on, Mr. Thief. Don't let this guy beat you! Tell him why he's wrong!

Japanese: ボクのコト怪人って呼んでくれるの ‥‥アナタだけです！
English: You're the only one who calls me "thief", Mr. Godot...

Japanese: ボク、やってみます！ ‥‥がんばりますッ！
English: ...Alright, I'll try! I-I'll do it! I will!

Japanese: ‥‥かなりヤル気になってるよ。
English: ...He's really got Mr. DeLite all worked up...

Japanese: ‥‥かなりヤル気をそがれたよ。
English: Yeah, he's like a kid at his first day of school...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　たしかに、ボク ‥‥脅迫状を受け取りました。
English: Mr. Wright! It's true that I received that blackmail letter.

Japanese: でもッ！
English: But...

Japanese: ジッサイにそこへ行ったかどうかは ベツの問題‥‥ですよね？
English: Whether or not I actually went there is a whole different story... right?

Japanese: あ。いや、どうなんだろ。 ボク、ヘンなコト言っちゃったかな
English: Oh, err, sorry if I'm sounding dramatic... I guess I don't know what I'm talking about

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ボウヤにしては 上出来、だぜ。
English: Ha...! That was brilliant!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうやら、もう１つ。 証拠品が必要のようですな。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright? It looks like you need some more evidence after all.

Japanese: （‥‥くそお、怪人め‥‥）
English: (Grrr! This stupid kid...!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 証拠品の提示を要求します。
English: Now then. Let's see your evidence.

Japanese: 脅迫状で呼び出された被告人が、 本当にＫＢ警備へ行った証拠を！
English: Demonstrate to this court that Mr. DeLite actually went to KB Security that night!

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: Here is your evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？ 説明してください、成歩堂さん。
English: ...What is this supposed to mean, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: え！　それは‥‥わかるでしょう？
English: Huh!? Well, um... Don't you know?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。オトコなら‥‥ 自分のケツは自分でふかないと。
English: Mr. Wright... If you're a real man, you should clean up your own mess.

Japanese: うぐぐ‥‥ッ！ （‥‥なんか、ハラ立つなあ）
English: Doh...! (For some reason, it bothers me more when HE says it!)

Japanese: 優作くん。これ‥‥、 きみのサイフだね？
English: Mr. DeLite. This wallet belongs to you, correct?

Japanese: あ！　‥‥は、はい。ボク、 落としちゃったんですよ、それ！
English: Ah! Y-Yes. It-It does. I-I had lost it somewhere!

Japanese: コラ、弁護人。拾ったらすぐ、 交番に届けなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! When you find a wallet, you should report it to the police right away!

Japanese: い‥‥いやいや。 いちおうコレ、証拠品ですから。
English: Ah! No! You don't understand. This is an important piece of evidence.
"Evidence"...?

Japanese: 優作くん。サイフを落としたの、 いつごろのコトですか？
English: Mr. DeLite... When did you first notice that you'd lost your wallet?

Japanese: ええと‥‥事件があった夜だと 思いますケド。
English: Err, let's see... I think it was on the night of the crime...

Japanese: まれかちゃんと晩ゴハンを食べに 行ったときは、まだあったから。
English: But I know I still had it when Dessie and I went out for dinner.

Japanese: このサイフは‥‥午前１時ごろ。 ＫＢ警備のビルで発見されました。
English: This wallet was found at approximately 1:00 AM at KB Security headquarters.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: What...!? Surely you're not serious!?

Japanese: ‥‥結論は、ただ１つ！
English: Yes... I am serious.

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、あの晩！ ＫＢ警備に行ったのです！
English: This proves that Mr. DeLite was, in fact, at KB Security that night!

Japanese: うわわうわわッ！
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥ロマンチストは哀しいぜ。 現実ってヤツを見ようとしねえ。
English: This is indeed sad... Romanticists are blind to the reality that lay before them.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: ...Huh? What does that mean?

Japanese: なぜ、このボウヤが警備会社で サイフなんて落とす必要がある？
English: Why did Mr. DeLite drop his wallet at KB Security? What purpose did it serve?

Japanese: まるで、ニセのアリバイを 作るため、ワザと置いたみたいに！
English: Perhaps it was dropped by his wife to create a false alibi for her dear husband instead!

Japanese: それは、きっと‥‥コイツを 使ったときに落としたんでしょう。
English: Sorry, but Mr. DeLite probably dropped it himself when he went to use this!

Japanese: ＫＢ警備会社の社長室に 入るためのカードです。
English: The key card to KB Security's CEO's office.

Japanese: しゃ、社長室‥‥だと‥‥？
English: CEO's... office?

Japanese: ‥‥おわかりになりましたか？
English: What's wrong? No snappy comebacks?

Japanese: 優作くんはあの晩の午前１時、 ＫＢ警備の社長室に入ったのです！
English: The fact is that Mr. DeLite entered the CEO's office around 1 o'clock that night!

Japanese: 優作くん。これ‥‥、 きみのサイフだね？
English: Mr. DeLite. This wallet belongs to you, correct?

Japanese: あ！　‥‥は、はい。ボク、 落としちゃったんですよ、それ！
English: Ah! Y-Yes. It-It does. I-I had lost it somewhere!

Japanese: コラ、弁護人。拾ったらすぐ、 交番に届けなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! When you find a wallet, you should report it to the police right away!

Japanese: い‥‥いやいや。 いちおうコレ、証拠品ですから。
English: Ah! No! You don't understand. This is an important piece of evidence.
"Evidence"...?

Japanese: 優作くん。サイフを落としたの、 いつごろのコトですか？
English: Mr. DeLite... When did you first notice that you'd lost your wallet?

Japanese: ええと‥‥事件があった夜だと 思いますケド。
English: Err, let's see... I think it was on the night of the crime...

Japanese: まれかちゃんと晩ゴハンを食べに 行ったときは、まだあったから。
English: But I know I still had it when Dessie and I went out for dinner.

Japanese: このサイフは‥‥午前１時ごろ。 ＫＢ警備のビルで発見されました。
English: This wallet was found at approximately 1:00 AM at KB Security headquarters.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: What...!? Surely you're not serious!?

Japanese: ‥‥結論は、ただ１つ！
English: Yes... I am serious.

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、あの晩！ ＫＢ警備に行ったのです！
English: This proves that Mr. DeLite was, in fact, at KB Security that night!

Japanese: うわわうわわッ！
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: 午前１時に、この被告人が 警備会社にいたのならば‥‥
English: So if the defendant was at KB Security at 1 o'clock in the morning...

Japanese: そのアリバイは、カンゼンに 成立します！
English: ...then that proves that he has a watertight alibi!

Japanese: ‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Noooooo!

Japanese: ちなみに、高菱屋百貨店から ＫＢ警備会社までのキョリは‥‥
English: Furthermore, considering the distance between Lordly Tailor and KB Security...

Japanese: クルマで約３０分もあるのです。 （‥‥と、矢張が言ってたな‥‥）
English: ...it would have taken 30 minutes to get there by car! (According to Larry anyway...)

Japanese: いかがですかッ、ゴドー検事！
English: Well, Mr. Godot!? Do you have anything to say?

Japanese: だから、コーヒーを飲まない！
English: And stop drinking that coffee!

Japanese: ‥‥オイ怪人。出番だぜ。 あの弁護士に、反論してやれよ！
English: ...Come on, Mr. Thief. Don't let this guy beat you! Tell him why he's wrong!

Japanese: ボクのコト怪人って呼んでくれるの ‥‥アナタだけです！
English: You're the only one who calls me "thief", Mr. Godot...

Japanese: ボク、やってみます！ ‥‥がんばりますッ！
English: ...Alright, I'll try! I-I'll do it! I will!

Japanese: ‥‥かなりヤル気になってるよ。
English: ...He's really got Mr. DeLite all worked up...

Japanese: ‥‥かなりヤル気をそがれたよ。
English: Yeah, he's like a kid at his first day of school...

Japanese: 不自然じゃないですか！ 警備会社でサイフを落とすなんて。
English: Look, it's just ridiculous! Why would I have dropped my wallet at KB Security!?

Japanese: きっと‥‥ボクをおとしいれる ために、ダレかが置いたんだ！
English: Someone must have planted it there to make it LOOK like I was there and not at the heist.

Japanese: “おとしいれる”って‥‥ ホント、メチャクチャ言うねー。
English: "Planted" it there? He's really reaching now...

Japanese: ‥‥優作くん。
English: Mr. DeLite.

Japanese: サイフは、きっと‥‥コイツを 使ったときに落としたんでしょう。
English: You probably dropped your wallet when you took it out to use this, didn't you?

Japanese: ＫＢ警備会社の社長室に 入るためのカードです。
English: The key card to KB Security's CEO's office.

Japanese: うううう‥‥！
English: Noooo!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　なかなか やるじゃねえか、まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! That was a pretty good try, Mr. Trite...

Japanese: だがな‥‥。まだ、 １つだけ、足りねえものがある。
English: Unfortunately, you've overlooked one small thing.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: Wh-What...?
"Motive" of course!

Japanese: この怪人には、夜中、警備会社に 行く理由なんかねえぜ！
English: Why would this thief go to KB Security in the middle of the night anyway!?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥どうやら、もう１つ。 証拠品が必要のようですな。
English: Hmm... Well, Mr. Wright? It looks like you need some more evidence after all.

Japanese: （‥‥くそお、怪人め‥‥）
English: (Grrr! This stupid kid...!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 証拠品の提示を要求します。
English: Now then. Let's see your evidence.

Japanese: 被告人が事件当夜の１時、ＫＢ警備 に行った理由を示す証拠を！
English: The evidence that shows why Mr. DeLite went to KB Security at 1 AM that night!

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: Here is your evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？ 説明してください、成歩堂さん。
English: ...What is this supposed to mean, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: え！　それは‥‥わかるでしょう？
English: Huh!? Well, um... Don't you know?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん。オトコなら‥‥ 自分のケツは自分でふかないと。
English: Mr. Wright... If you're a real man, you should clean up your own mess.

Japanese: うぐぐ‥‥ッ！ （‥‥なんか、ハラ立つなあ）
English: Doh...! (For some reason, it bothers me more when HE says it!)

Japanese: 被告人。‥‥これ、 モチロン知っていますね？
English: Mr. DeLite... I believe you've seen this before, correct?

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！　それは‥‥！
English: Ah...! Th-That's...!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: 脅迫状です。 ‥‥そう書いてありますから。
English: A blackmail letter. That's what it looks like from the contents.

Japanese: きょうはくじょう‥‥だと？
English: B-Blackmail...?

Japanese: 中身をカンタンに紹介すると、 《５００万円もってこい》ですね。
English: Yes, basically it says, "bring $50,000 dollars".

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ それは、リッパな脅迫ですな‥‥
English: Hmm... That certainly sounds like blackmail alright...

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、怪人の犯行時間、 脅迫者と取り引きしていたのです！
English: At the time of the theft, Mr. DeLite was dealing with the blackmailer himself...

Japanese: 現場からクルマで３０分のキョリが ある、ＫＢ警備会社の社長室で！
English: ...in KB Security's CEO office, a full half an hour away from the scene of the crime!
...Ungh... Urk...

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: No No No No No NOOOOOOOOOO OOOOOOOOOOO!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィッ！
English: Order! Order! OOOOORDER!!

Japanese: ‥‥犯行のあった時間、 被告人はＫＢ警備会社にいた‥‥
English: So when the theft of the urn occurred, the defendant was at KB Security...

Japanese: どうやら、弁護側は、 カンペキに立証したようです！
English: It looks like a perfect case for the defense!

Japanese: ‥‥その“カンペキな立証” とやらも‥‥
English: ...You may see it as a perfect case, Judge, but to me...

Japanese: このゴドー・ブレンド１０７号の 味わいほどではなかったようだぜ。
English: Well, let's just say that my Godot Blend #107 impresses me a lot more.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Wh-What are you trying to say?

Japanese: ‥‥怪人が、警備会社の 社長に脅迫されていた‥‥
English: You say the thief was being blackmailed by the CEO of a security company...

Japanese: アンタ、その社長さんを キチンと調べたのかい？
English: But... Did you actually investigate this CEO at all?

Japanese: え！　そ、それは‥‥まだ‥‥
English: Huh!? Well, umm... No... I guess not.

Japanese: 面識のない男を“脅迫者”呼ばわり するの‥‥どうかと思うぜ。
English: Accusing a man of blackmail with no proof? I'm not sure what I think of that...

Japanese: （面識があるオトコを怪人呼ばわり するのも、どうかと思うけど）
English: ("Not sure what I think of that?"... At least I know what I think of you...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですね‥‥。
English: Hmm... Good point. I'm not sure what I think of it myself...

Japanese: “被告人・天杉　優作が、 社長室に入った”‥‥
English: You claim that the defendant entered the CEO's office...

Japanese: ‥‥これがジジツだと、証言できる 証人が必要でしょう。
English: ...but you will need at least one witness to corroborate your claim, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　いっちょ、 その社長さんを探してこようか？
English: Nick! I think we're going to have to track down the CEO guy...

Japanese: その必要はないよ。
English: No, we don't have to track down the CEO at all.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？ まるほどうさんよ。
English: What do you mean by that, Mr. Trite?

Japanese: ‥‥ある人物に、それを証言できる 可能性があります。
English: There is someone else who can testify.

Japanese: “事件当夜の午前１時、カードが 使用された”と証言できる人物！
English: This is the person who can testify that the key card was used at 1 AM that night!

Japanese: ‥‥今日はじめて、アンタの ジョークがおもしろいと感じたぜ。
English: Ha...! That's the first time one of your jokes actually made me truly laugh.

Japanese: （今日はまだ、一度もジョークを 言ったおぼえがないぞ‥‥）
English: (Umm, I haven't actually made any jokes... Not intentionally anyway...)

Japanese: 私には、今の冗談のオモシロさが 今ひとつ、ピンと来ませんな。
English: Well, perhaps you find it funny, but I fail to see any humor in it!

Japanese: （問題は‥‥優作くんのサイフに 入っていた、キーカードだ）
English: (The problem is the key card that was in Ron's wallet...)

Japanese: （午前１時に、あのカードが使用 されたことを立証できればいい）
English: (Somehow I need to prove that the card was used at 1 o'clock in the morning.)

Japanese: ダレですか‥‥この、 たよりなさそうな青年は。
English: Wh-Who is this useless-looking young man?

Japanese: 見おぼえ、ありませんか？
English: You don't remember him, Your Honor?

Japanese: うううん‥‥そうですね‥‥
English: Hmmmm... Not exactly...

Japanese: なぜか、見ていると イヤな汗がニジんできますな。
English: But just looking at his picture makes the bile start to rise in my throat.

Japanese: おぼえてないみたいだね。 ‥‥２年前の事件。
English: It looks like he doesn't remember the case from 2 years ago...

Japanese: キオクを封印しちまったのかも しれないな。
English: He probably blocked out that memory on purpose.

Japanese: ‥‥彼は事件当夜、ＫＢ警備で ガードマンをしていました。
English: Anyway, this man was working as a guard at KB Security that night.

Japanese: ‥‥ガードマン‥‥？
問題は‥‥、このキーカードです。
English: The question at hand is this key card.

Japanese: ああ。そいつはウチの ビルで使われているカードだぜ。
English: Yup. That's the key card they use in the building I work.

Japanese: シリアル番号によると‥‥ 社長室のカードだな。
English: According to the serial number, this one is for the CEO's office.

Japanese: 部屋に入るときに必要でさ。 使うと、記録が残るんだ。
English: You need it to get into that room and every time you use that card, it leaves a record.

Japanese: ダレが何時に部屋を出入りしたか、 バッチリってワケ。
English: Yeah, it tells you exactly who entered the room and when.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥そう！　社長さんを 調べるまでもありません！
English: As you can see, there's no need to investigate the CEO of KB Security!

Japanese: キーカードのデータを調べれば、 真相はハッキリするはずですッ！
English: We should be able to discover the truth simply by analyzing this key card's data!

Japanese: うぶぐほおおお‥‥ッ！
English: Bwaaaah...!

Japanese: いかがでしたか？　ゴドー検事。
English: Well, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: ＫＢ警備会社の社長の名前は、 毒島　黒兵衛（ぶすじまくろべえ）
English: The name of the CEO of KB Security is Kane Bullard.

Japanese: ‥‥そいつと連絡をとるのは ムリだったが‥‥
English: I was unable to contact him directly, but...

Japanese: キーカードのデータは提出させた。 ‥‥コイツだ。
English: I got the key card data. ...Here.

Japanese: そ、それで‥‥、そのデータは！ 結果は、どうだったのですか！
English: S-So what does it show!?

Japanese: このキーカードのシリアル番号が、 使用記録と一致した。
English: Each key card has its own serial number and they leave detailed records of their use.

Japanese: カードが使用された時間は ‥‥事件当夜の、午前１時。
English: According to this data... this card was used at 1 AM on the morning of the crime.

Japanese: 証拠品《キーカード》の データを書きなおした。
English: Key Card updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: それでは‥‥仮面マスクとして この写真に写るのは、不可能です！
English: But that means... it can't be Mr. DeLite dressed as Mask☆DeMasque in this photo!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ たしかに、そのとおりだろうぜ。
English: Ha...! It looks like you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥たった２分じゃア、ウマい コーヒーをいれるヒマもねえ‥‥
English: Two minutes isn't even enough time to brew a good cup o' Joe...

Japanese: じゃ‥‥じゃあ‥‥！
English: So... So then...!

Japanese: 被告人、天杉　優作は‥‥事件当時 ＫＢ警備会社の社長室にいた。
English: Ron DeLite was clearly in the office of KB Security's CEO at the time of the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥検察側は‥‥ それを事実として認めるぜ。
English: ...The prosecutor's office is ready to admit that fact.

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、 仮面マスクじゃなかった、ってな。
English: Therefore, it's impossible for the defendant to be Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: やったよ、なるほどくんっ！
English: Good job! You did it, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで！
English: ...That's enough!

Japanese: ワレワレはもう少しで、無実の 青年に汚名を着せるところでした。
English: I came perilously close to besmirching the record of an innocent young man.

Japanese: ‥‥おぞましき“怪人”の汚名を。
English: Besmirching him with the title of "thief"!

Japanese: ど、どうしました？　裁判長。
English: Wh-What's wrong, Your Honor?

Japanese: これから判決を下しますけど‥‥ 今度こそ、異議はありませんよね？
English: I'm ready to pass judgment... But before I do that, do you have any further objections?

Japanese: はいッ！
English: No, Your Honor!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
English: Hrpmh...!

Japanese: うううううう‥‥
English: Unnnggh...

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: では、被告人・天杉　優作に 判決を言いわたしましょう。
English: The court finds the defendant, Mr. Ron DeLite...

Japanese: ‥‥では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: Court is now adjourned!

Japanese: 同日　午後　２時２４分 地方裁判所　被告人第４控え室
English: October 13, 2:24 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 4

Japanese: やっぱり、なるほどくんが 正しかったんだね！
English: Nick! You did it! You were right after all!

Japanese: あたし、ちょっと恥ずかしいよ。
English: Actually... I'm a little bit ashamed of myself.

Japanese: リューイチくん！
English: Nicky boy!

Japanese: あ。まれかさん‥‥。
English: Oh! Ms. DeLite...!

Japanese: アタシ、信じてたよ、 リューイチくんのコト！
English: I knew you could do it! I believed in you all along, Nicky boy!

Japanese: ‥‥カンシャしてる。ホントよ。
English: ...I don't know how I can ever repay you.

Japanese: は、はあ、どうも‥‥ （カオが赤くなるな‥‥）
English: Aww... Shucks... Thanks, Ms. DeLite. (I just know I'm blushing...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ おめでとうございますっ！
English: Congratulations, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: あ！　は、春美ちゃん‥‥ （イヤな予感がする‥‥）
English: Oh! P-Pearls...! (I've got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: まあっ！ どなたですか、その女性は！
English: *gasp!* Wh-Who is this woman!

Japanese: い、いや、ベツにその。 このヒトは‥‥
English: Oh, she... She's nobody... She's just ahh...

Japanese: 真宵さまの前で！　他の女性と！ いいムードに‥‥！
English: You're blushing! How dare you do this in front of Mystic Maya!

Japanese: 恥をお知りなさいッ！
English: You should be ashamed of yourself!

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ビンタされた）
English: (Yeowch! She slapped me...)

Japanese: あの、はみちゃん。 ‥‥このひとはね。
English: Umm, Pearly...

Japanese: 奥さんなんだよ。 天杉　優作さんの。
English: This woman is Ms. Desirée DeLite. She's our client's wife.

Japanese: まあっ！　なるほどくんっ！
English: *gasp!* Mr. Nick!

Japanese: は、はいッ！
English: Y-Yes!?

Japanese: ‥‥なんという‥‥ ヒトさまの奥方にっ！
English: You're even worse than I thought! Going behind the back of your own client...!

Japanese: ちちちち、ちが
English: N-N-No! You've got it all wrong!

Japanese: ユルしませんっ！
English: I'll never forgive you!

Japanese: （うううう‥‥ 往復ビンタされた‥‥）
English: (Owww... A double slap...)

Japanese: それにしても、よかったねー。
English: Well, anyway. All's well that ends well, right?

Japanese: 倉院のツボは戻ったし、 怪人はつかまったし！
English: We got the Sacred Urn back and the thief has been caught!

Japanese: そうだねー。 リューイチくんのおかげだよ。
English: You're so right! And it's all thanks to Nicky boy here!

Japanese: でも、ツボを取り戻してくれたの、 まれかさんじゃないですか！
English: But actually it was you, Ms. DeLite, that brought us our urn back!

Japanese: ありがとうございました！
English: Thank you from the bottom of my heart!

Japanese: ははっ。‥‥いやいや。 テレちゃうね。こういうの。
English: Oh, please... You're embarrassing me!

Japanese: （‥‥と、事件が解決したのに）
English: (If we won the case...)

Japanese: （どうして浮かないカオ してるんだよ、このヒトは‥‥）
English: (...then why does this guy still look so glum...?)

Japanese: ううう‥‥怪人、ボクなのに。 ‥‥いや、でも。むしろそれは今は
English: Ugggh... But I am the thief... Actually, what's the point now

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたの？
English: ...What is it, honey?

Japanese: アタシ、ユーサクくんのために がんばったんだよ！
English: I did my best for you, Ronnie!

Japanese: う、うん。それは‥‥とても ウレシイよ、まれかちゃん。
English: I-I know that and I appreciate it, Dessie...
But the thing is...

Japanese: ‥‥でも、 よろこんでばかりもいられない。
English: Come on... Give the kid some time.

Japanese: そう。それこそが人生のブルース、 ってヤツさ‥‥なあ、相棒！
English: He's just got a little touch of the blues. You know about feeling blue, right, amigo?

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！ どうしてここへ！
English: M-Mr. Godot! What are you doing here!?

Japanese: なあに‥‥オレの相棒に ヒトコト、礼を言おうと思ってな。
English: Oh come on! I just came here to say thanks to my newest buddy...

Japanese: ‥‥まるほどうさんよォ。
English: ...You, Mr. Trite.

Japanese: （“アイボー”呼ばわりする前に、 名前おぼえろよ‥‥）
English: (Maybe you should learn my name before you call me "buddy"...)

Japanese: ‥‥さて。お遊びはここまでだ。
English: Well, playtime is over.
Huh...?

Japanese: 今朝、早く‥‥ 毒島　黒兵衛の死体が発見された。
English: Early this morning... the body of Kane Bullard was discovered.

Japanese: ぶすじま‥‥？　最近、 どこかで聞いたような名前だね。
English: Kane Bullard...? Where have I heard that name before?

Japanese: それって‥‥ ＫＢ警備の社長さんじゃない！
English: Isn't that... the name of the CEO of KB Security?
B-Body...?

Japanese: 死亡推定時刻は‥‥ ９月１２日の午前１時ごろだ。
English: The estimated time of death was 1 AM on October 12th.

Japanese: ９月１２日の午前１時‥‥？ そ、それって‥‥まさか！
English: 1 AM on October 12th...? Y-You don't mean!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだよ、相棒。
English: ...That's right, amigo.

Japanese: 怪人がケチなツボを盗んでいたとき ＫＢ警備会社では‥‥
English: At the same time that a cheap little urn was being stolen...

Japanese: ヒトが１人、死んでいたのさ。
English: ...the CEO of KB Security was being murdered.

Japanese: そ、それで‥‥ なぜ、ここへ‥‥？
English: S-So then... What are you doing here?

Japanese: わかってるだろう？　相棒‥‥
English: Oh come on. You've figured it out already, haven't you amigo? Or...

Japanese: オレたちが今日、協力して 証明したコト‥‥お忘れかい？
English: Have you already forgotten about that piece of info I helped you out with today?

Japanese: （“協力”‥‥だって？）
English: ("Helped me out"? What!?)

Japanese: ９月１２日、午前１時。
English: On October 12th, at 1 o'clock in the morning...

Japanese: 天杉　優作は、ＫＢ警備会社の 社長室にいたんだ‥‥殺人現場に。
English: Ron DeLite was in the CEO's office... ...The scene of the murder.

Japanese: 被害者から脅迫状で呼び出されて ‥‥やりきれない殺意を抱いて。
English: After getting that blackmail letter, he must have been imbrued with utter rage.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ ‥‥さ、さつい‥‥？
English: Wh-What are you saying!? Imbrued with rage...?

Japanese: まさか‥‥ 知らねえとは言わせねえぜ。
English: Come on! Don't tell me you didn't know it!

Japanese: 天杉　優作はムカシ、 ＫＢ警備会社の社員‥‥
English: Ron DeLite was once an employee of KB Security...

Japanese: プロの警備員だったんだ。
English: He was a professional security guard.

Japanese: （ＫＢ警備の‥‥社員！）
English: (An employee of KB Security!?)

Japanese: ‥‥アリバイってのは、 “現場不在証明”のコトだが‥‥
English: It looks like the alibi that saved him from being convicted as a thief...

Japanese: 今日の裁判で、オレたちは‥‥ “逆アリバイ”を立証したのさ。
English: ...is going to be the noose that gets him hanged. Kind of an "anti-alibi".

Japanese: ぎゃく‥‥
English: No way...

Japanese: 『被告人は怪人ではない。なぜなら 犯行時刻、別の現場にいたから』
English: He can't be the thief because he was at the murder scene when the murder occurred!

Japanese: う、ウソよ！　‥‥そんな‥‥
English: N-No! Th-That's a lie! It can't be true!

Japanese: あ、あうう‥‥、ぼ、ボク‥‥ ボクは‥‥怪人であって、その。
English: Oh! Ohhhh! B-But I... I am a thief I tell you!

Japanese: 天杉　優作。アンタの身柄を 引きつづき拘留するぜ。
English: Ron DeLite. You're going back to prison again.

Japanese: 罪名はちょっと変わるがな。 ‥‥殺人容疑、に。
English: This time the charge is much more serious. This time you'll be tried for murder.

Japanese: そんな‥‥そんなバカな‥‥
English: Wh-What!? This can't... This is impossible!

Japanese: 弁護士さんよォ‥‥ オレをガッカリさせるな。
English: I'm looking forward to another exciting showdown, Mr. Trite!

Japanese: アンタの“実力”ってヤツは‥‥
English: You and I aren't through with each other yet.

Japanese: まさか、このテイドだって ワケじゃねえよなあ。
English: Surely you won't back down from a challenge! You've never been a coward!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　このけんじさん ‥‥お知り合いなのですか‥‥？
English: Mr. Nick! Is there something personal between you two...?

Japanese: オレはアンタと戦うために、 地獄から戻ってきたんだ。
English: I've returned from the depths of Hell to do battle with you.

Japanese: ‥‥もっと、 楽しませてくれないとなァ‥‥。
English: ...At least let me have some fun while I'm here!

Japanese: （このオトコ‥‥ いったい、ダレなんだ！）
English: (This guy... Who the heck is he!?)

Japanese: （静かな‥‥だけど、深い “敵意”を感じるぞ‥‥）
English: (He may be quiet... But he's the most dangerous enemy I've ever faced...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥、天杉はつれていくぜ。
English: Well then... Time to say goodbye to Mr. DeLite.

Japanese: な、なるほどくん‥‥ ‥‥いったい、どうなってるの？
English: N-Nick...! How could this be happening?

Japanese: ぼくたちの依頼人が、目の前で タイホされたんだ。殺人容疑で。
English: Right in front of our very eyes, our client has been arrested for murder.

Japanese: その罪をカンペキに立証したのは ‥‥ぼくたち自身、なんだよ。
English: And the one who established his presence at the scene... was me.

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！ ユーサクくうううううううううん！
English: EEEEEYYYAAAAHHH! R-RONNNIEEEEEEEE!

Japanese: （無罪判決を受けた天杉　優作が、 殺人容疑でタイホ‥‥）
English: (Arrested for murder on the very same day he's declared innocent of larceny...)

Japanese: （いったい‥‥ これから、どうなるんだ！）
English: (What the heck's going to happen next!?)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
